content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 30,
1992
Marjorie Baxter
Assistant City Attorney II
City of Simi Valley
2929 Tapo Canyon Road
Simi Valley,
CA
93063
RE:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-92-210
Dear Ms.
Baxter:
You have requested assistance under the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
!
Because
your request is a general inquiry,
we treat it as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).
2
!
You have asked whether a Planning Commissioner must include
payments received from the City as income on the Form 721
(Statement of Economic Interests)
for a League of California
Cities conference the Commissioner attended.
As stated in the instructions for disclosing income on
the Form 721, travel payments received from a state, local,
or
federal government agency,
for which equal or greater
consideration has been provided,
are not required to be disclosed.
Generally,
when a government agency pays travel costs for an
employee, it is presumed that equal or greater consideration has
been provided by the employee.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015..
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et
All references to regulations are to
Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion orformal written advice.
(Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c).)
4281 Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (9I6 22-566C


Marjorie Baxter
Page
2
I hope this clarifies the Planning Commissioner’s filing
obligations.
Please contact me at
(916)
322—5662 if you have any
additional questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
op,
By:
Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: You have asked whether a Planning Commissioner must include payments received from the City as income on the Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) for a League of California Cities conference the Commissioner attended.
sections.conclusion: As stated in the instructions for disclosing income on the Form 721, travel payments received from a state, local, or federal government agency, for which equal or greater consideration has been provided, are not required to be disclosed. Generally, when a government agency pays travel costs for an employee, it is presumed that equal or greater consideration has been provided by the employee.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Planning Commissioner must disclose on Form 721 payments received from the City for attendance at a League of California Cities conference.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Travel payments received from a government agency for which equal or greater consideration has been provided are not required to be disclosed on Form 721, and it is presumed that a government agency providing travel costs for an employee has received equal or greater consideration.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document clearly formatted as FPPC advice letter with question and conclusion sections identifiable. Marked as informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c), which limits immunity protections. No withdrawal or declination language present.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked whether a Planning Commissioner must include payments received from the City as income on the Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) for a League of California Cities conference the Commissioner attended.

CONCLUSION: As stated in the instructions for disclosing income on the Form 721, travel payments received from a state, local, or federal government agency, for which equal or greater consideration has been provided, are not required to be disclosed. Generally, when a government agency pays travel costs for an employee, it is presumed that equal or greater consideration has been provided by the employee.

Because your request is a general inquiry, we treat it as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c). 2 ! You have asked whether a Planning Commissioner must include payments received from the City as income on the Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) for a League of California Cities conference the Commissioner attended. As stated in the instructions for disclosing income on the Form 721, travel payments received from a state, local, or federal government agency, for which equal or greater consideration has been provided, are not required to be disclosed. Generally, when a government agency pays travel costs for an employee, it is presumed that equal or greater consideration has been provided by the employee. 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.. embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to a City of Simi Valley Assistant City Attorney's question about whether a Planning Commissioner must disclose City-paid travel costs for a League of California Cities conference on Form 721. The FPPC concludes that such travel payments do not require disclosure under the applicable Form 721 instructions.
